perturbation.original_text: 3.6.1 All payments shall be made by bank transfer to such account as may be indicated by Dexcel, Dexcel and Kitov each bearing their own bank transfer costs, net thirty (30) days from Delivery. All payments shall be made in U.S. Dollars.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.6.1 All payments shall be made by bank transfer to such account as may be indicated by Dexcel, Dexcel and Kitov each bearing their own bank transfer costs, net sixty (60) days from Delivery. All payments shall be made in U.S. Dollars.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by modifying the payment deadline from 'net thirty (30) days from Delivery' to 'net sixty (60) days from Delivery.' This creates uncertainty and potential disputes since Section 3.6.2 refers to 21 working days late payment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.6.2 With the exception of amounts in legitimate dispute, in the event that Kitov is more than twenty one (21) Working Days late in meeting the payment schedule set forth in Section ​3.6.1, Dexcel may, upon seven (7) Working Days' written notice to Kitov (i) delay the delivery of Product ordered until the amounts in arrears are paid, (ii) charge penalties on late payment with interest at the rate of **** per month from the due date for payment until payment is actually made, and/or (iii) change or limit the terms of payment for future orders, including requiring the prepayment for new orders or the provision of a letter of credit by Kitov (at Kitov's expense) from a bank reasonably acceptable to Dexcel.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment deadline in Section 3.6.1 from 'net thirty (30) days from Delivery' to 'net sixty (60) days from Delivery.' This directly contradicts Section 3.6.2, which refers to a 'twenty one (21) Working Days late' payment schedule based on the original 'thirty (30) days' deadline. The change creates a direct conflict and potential ambiguity regarding when penalties for late payment are applicable, as the original 21-day late payment window is now inconsistent with the new 60-day payment term. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines' and leads to disputes over contractual obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 The Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and remain in full force and effect for an initial term of **** from the Supply Commencement Date of the Product ("Initial Term"). Following the Initial Term, the Agreement shall automatically be renewed for additional periods of **** (each, a "Renewal Term," and, together with the Initial Term, the "Term")), unless a Party provides written notification of non-renewal to the other Party at least **** of the Initial Term or a Renewal Term.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 The Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and remain in full force and effect for an initial term of **** from the Supply Commencement Date of the Product ("Initial Term"). Following the Initial Term, the Agreement shall automatically be renewed for additional periods of **** (each, a "Renewal Term," and, together with the Initial Term, the "Term")), unless a Party provides written notification of non-renewal to the other Party at least ninety (90) days of the Initial Term or a Renewal Term.
perturbation.explanation: Here, the clause states the period to provide written notification of non-renewal, here, being contradict with Termination rights, described on section 5.3. This change introduces in-text contradiction, specifically conflicting the time frame for providing termination notice with the notice related to Change of Control, since the deadline of the section 5.1 being changed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.3 in the event of a Change of Control, the Party which was not subject to the Change of Control may terminate this Agreement upon six (6) months advance written notification. The affected Party is obligated to notify the other Party of its decision to terminate within thirty (30) days following notice of the Change of Control.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5.1 modifies the notification period for non-renewal to 'at least ninety (90) days'. Section 5.3 states that in the event of a Change of Control, the unaffected party may terminate the agreement with 'six (6) months advance written notification', but the affected party only has 'thirty (30) days following notice of the Change of Control' to notify the other party of its decision to terminate. This creates a direct conflict, because the 30-day notification period in Section 5.3 is inconsistent with the 90-day notification period for non-renewal introduced in the changed text of Section 5.1, thus creating an inconsistency as per the definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: 11.1 The provisions of this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the Parties hereto and their respective successors and assigns. Notwithstanding the aforesaid, either Party shall be entitled to assign, delegate, and/or subcontract its rights and obligation under this Agreement, in whole or in part, to one or more of its Affiliates on prior written notice to the other Party. For purposes of this Agreement, any merger, consolidation, or change of corporate structure following which there is a Change of Control of Kitov shall be considered as an assignment by Kitov, allowing Dexcel to terminate the Agreement as heretofore provided.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.1 The provisions of this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the Parties hereto and their respective successors and assigns. Notwithstanding the aforesaid, neither Party shall be entitled to assign, delegate, and/or subcontract its rights and obligation under this Agreement, in whole or in part, to any of its Affiliates without the prior explicit written consent of the other Party, which consent may be withheld at the other Party's sole discretion. For purposes of this Agreement, any merger, consolidation, or change of corporate structure following which there is a Change of Control of Kitov shall be considered as an assignment by Kitov, allowing Dexcel to terminate the Agreement as heretofore provided.
perturbation.explanation: This alteration establishes in-text contradiction by precluding a party from delegating, assigning, or subcontracting duties to affiliates without explicit prior written consent from the counterparty. This contradicts and causes uncertainty in the section 2.2, related to Kitov's responsibilities for costs related to their Affiliates.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 Kitov shall be responsible (itself or through its Affiliates and Distributors) for all costs related to the maintenance of or changes to the Specifications, materials, suppliers of the API and/or other materials used for the manufacture or Packaging of the Product, regulatory dossiers, and/or the Marketing Authorizations for the Product. Dexcel shall provide any and all reasonable assistance to Kitov in this respect during the Term.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed assignment to affiliates with prior written notice, while the changed text requires explicit prior written consent from the other party, which can be withheld at their sole discretion. This directly contradicts Section 2.2, which states Kitov is responsible for costs through its Affiliates, creating a contradiction because Kitov may not be able to use Affiliates if Dexcel withholds consent, impacting Kitov's ability to fulfill its responsibilities outlined in 2.2. This creates a direct inconsistency regarding the conditions under which Kitov can utilize affiliates.
